agent_notes: Islington has enough PAINTS-coded drinks, galleries and markets for a strong longlist, but Books & Coffee is thin under the v6 on-site/next-door coffee rule.
Creative Walks are deliberately sparse: Islington parks are not on the PAINTS safety list, so the only included walk is the explicitly permitted Regent's Canal Camden to Angel stretch.
Public sources rarely prove group walk-in tolerance for 8-12; those cases are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: Cocktail bar open afternoons/evenings per venue and listings; exact 2026 holiday hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public booking pages and listings indicate bar service, but group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Booths and lounge seating can suit a small social group if arriving early; host should call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Small cocktail bar; avoid peak Friday/Saturday late evening.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu 18:00 or Sat 16:30 before the room fills.
venues.why_fits_paints: A neighbourhood cocktail room with a deliberately theatrical, conversation-friendly feel. It has enough personality for show-and-tell without becoming a loud club. The Callooh Callay lineage gives it a story attendees can remember.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in 2016 by the Callooh Callay team, bringing Shoreditch-style cocktail craft into a smaller Angel neighbourhood setting.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, wine, small plates.
venues.safety_notes: Upper Street/Angel has strong evening footfall and direct tube access; normal late-night city awareness applies.
venues.backup_walk_time: 6 min walk via Islington Green/Colebrooke Row
venues.risks_watchouts: Potential obviousness penalty from press/listicle visibility; group seating should be confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Evening cocktail service; exact current hours and bank-holiday treatment UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Known as a destination cocktail bar with reservations; walk-in/group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, so score is held below its cultural profile.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely requires advance contact for 8-12; not suitable for casual peak-hour arrival.
venues.capacity_notes: Intimate room; host should only use with confirmed group accommodation.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a small bar with a large London cocktail mythology. It gives a group something to talk about immediately: drinks as experiments, not just orders. The room is intimate enough for strangers to hear each other.
venues.anecdote_story: Tony Conigliaro's bar became famous for molecular and culinary cocktail thinking from a quiet Islington street.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Experimental cocktails and classic bar serves.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet residential street immediately off Angel; use Angel station routes after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation dependence and high reputation make walk-in operation fragile.

venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; public source does not prove 8-12 walk-ins. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: More operationally robust than smaller cocktail rooms; likely best with a heads-up call.
venues.capacity_notes: Wine bar and kitchen with multiple tables; group table plausibly feasible off-peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Its wine-shop intelligence gives hosts easy prompts around bottle stories and small producers. It is less hidden than ideal but warm, practical and reliable. The list supports show-and-tell without needing a formal tasting.
venues.anecdote_story: Humble Grape built its identity around importing from small, often family-run producers rather than generic supermarket-style wine.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine by glass/bottle, small plates, retail bottles.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Angel retail street network; good onward transport.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small chain feel compared with more singular PAINTS picks; confirm walk-in handling.

venues.opening_hours: Trullo site lists Trullo Bar Mon-Thu from 18:00, Fri-Sat from 17:30, Sundays on bank holiday weekends.
venues.walk_in_policy: No-bookings/walk-in model reported by press; group 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm because the bar is tiny.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 6, fragile for 8-12 unless very early or split around counter/table.
venues.capacity_notes: Hot Dinners reported about 12-15 seats; operationally tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: A candlelit micro wine bar attached to a beloved neighbourhood restaurant is exactly the right kind of small discovery. The wine and snack format makes it conversational. It is PAINTS-coded but capacity-limited.
venues.anecdote_story: The bar replaced Trullo's shop and turned a tiny adjacent space into a European wine counter.
venues.serve_or_on_view: European wines and Italian-leaning snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Highbury Corner has strong transport links; avoid lingering in the station underpasses late.
venues.risks_watchouts: Very small; demote/cancel if host cannot secure early arrival plan.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram lists Tue-Sat 17:00-23:00; exact event-date handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: OpenTable/Instagram indicate bookings and walk-ins; group 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small venue; feasible only early with heads-up.
venues.capacity_notes: Intimate wine bar and deli; avoid peak service.
venues.why_fits_paints: The wine-and-wax positioning gives it a distinct sensory hook: records, bottles and small plates. It is neighbourhood-scale rather than destination-slick. The compactness is part of the charm and the risk.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself around Wine & Wax: a record-culture-inflected wine bar/shop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Low-intervention and classic wines, seasonal small plates, coffee and beer.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Highbury route with reasonable station access; daylight/early evening preferred.
venues.risks_watchouts: Public hours conflict between sources; host must verify current schedule.

venues.opening_hours: Official site confirms active booking/menus; third-party press lists daily lunch/dinner patterns. Exact current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says walk-ins welcome; 8-12 walk-in likely difficult because tables are in high demand. Host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: More dinner-pub than drinks bar; works as a show-and-tell pub only with early arrival or arranged table.
venues.capacity_notes: Small converted pub; high popularity means operational risk.
venues.why_fits_paints: A converted corner pub with South Indian food gives the group an immediate story and sensory anchor. It is culturally strong but operationally hot. Best as a backup-flavoured drinks event, not a pure walk-in bar.
venues.anecdote_story: Formerly the Cuckoo Inn, transformed by chef Prince Durairaj into a South Indian pub that helped define London's desi-pub moment.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Beer, cocktails, South Indian pub food.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Caledonian Road/Barnsbury area; advise attendees to use main-road routes to Overground or Highbury & Islington.
venues.risks_watchouts: Popularity and reservations make group walk-in fragile; capped at Tier B.

venues.opening_hours: Official about page lists Mon 17:00-22:00, Tue-Fri 12:00-22:00, Sat 11:00-22:00, Sun 11:00-21:30.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says walk-ins always welcome; 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Two-floor pub makes group fit more plausible than The Tamil Prince, but still confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Converted Victorian pub with dining and bar spaces.
venues.why_fits_paints: It gives the Tamil Prince story a more pub-forward, lower-friction setting. The converted boozer atmosphere is warm and distinctive. Still, it is food-led enough to sit just below the strongest drinks picks.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened by the Tamil Prince team in a Victorian pub, with South Indian roots and a more casual pub offer.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, pints, curries and small plates.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet Barnsbury side street; keep arrivals/departures on main lit roads.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tripadvisor sentiment is mixed; host should check recent reviews before programming.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Wed-Thu 16:00-23:00, Fri 16:00-00:00, Sat 11:00-00:00, Sun 11:00-22:00; another official brunch page lists Mon-Thu 16:00-23:00, so host must verify.
venues.walk_in_policy: Eventbrite/live-music listing says walk-ins welcome for some events; 8-12 group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Pub format can handle groups better than microbars, but tables should be checked.
venues.capacity_notes: Canal-side gastropub with music; noise varies.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Louisiana pub on the canal gives a genuine story and a rare London flavour. It is warm, specific and less predictable than a generic Angel pub. Mixed review signal keeps it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Chef Jacob Kenedy's family connection to the Plaquemine Lock in Louisiana gives the pub its name and backstory.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cajun/Creole food, beer, wine, cocktails, live music.
venues.safety_notes: Canal-adjacent; keep group movement on lit streets after dark rather than towpath lingering.
venues.risks_watchouts: Rating/sentiment appears below strongest PAINTS floor on some sources; host should re-check Google before use.

venues.opening_hours: Time Out lists noon-23:00 Mon-Sat and noon-22:30 Sun; Michelin lists Tue-Sat hours. Current exact hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub service implies walk-ins, dining bookings available; group 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Classic pub layout can work off-peak; avoid dining-rush arrival.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub with food residency; space can be tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Canonbury pub with literary myth and serious food is exactly the sort of familiar-but-storied venue PAINTS can animate. It is more characterful than polished. Works best when the host leans into the pub-history angle.
venues.anecdote_story: Often linked to George Orwell's ideal pub essay, The Moon Under Water, giving hosts a natural opener about imagined perfect pubs.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and current kitchen residency food.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Canonbury; straightforward lit walk to Highbury & Islington.
venues.risks_watchouts: Kitchen/operator details change; confirm current residency and opening.

venues.opening_hours: Official market page lists Wednesday and Saturday 09:00-18:00, with possible Thu/Fri/Sun stalls subject to change.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access outdoor market; N/A for market. Meeting point walk-in: Kipferl/independent cafes nearby UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a roaming group; meet at Angel/Islington Green then browse in pairs.
venues.capacity_notes: Pedestrian passage can become narrow on Saturdays; keep group moving.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Islington's strongest market-day fit: car-free, antique/vintage-led, and visually distinctive. It gives participants objects to react to rather than stalls to simply consume. The street itself carries enough old Angel texture for a two-hour route.
venues.anecdote_story: Camden Passage is a long-standing antiques and vintage enclave tucked just off Upper Street, with separate stall areas and arcades.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antiques, vintage clothing, jewellery, collectables, bric-a-brac, cafes.
venues.safety_notes: High daytime footfall near Angel; suitable for daytime market format.
venues.risks_watchouts: Some stalls vary by day and weather; use Wednesday/Saturday core market days.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Wed/Sat/Sun antiques and collectables 08:30-17:30, Thu vintage fashion 09:00-18:00, Fri collectables 08:30-17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market. Meeting-point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good, if treated as a slow browse rather than a single huddle.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered/arcade elements improve weather resilience but aisles can pinch.
venues.why_fits_paints: It offers a more object-rich, covered version of the Camden Passage experience. Vintage and collectables create natural show-and-tell prompts. Its proximity to cafes makes it operationally easy.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of the Camden Passage antiques ecosystem, with different market personalities by day.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antiques, collectables, vintage fashion, books depending on day.
venues.safety_notes: Central Angel, daytime, high pedestrian activity.
venues.risks_watchouts: Day-specific stall mix; host should choose the day based on desired antique/vintage emphasis.

venues.opening_hours: Islington Life lists Tue-Sat 09:00-18:00 and Sun 08:30-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; N/A for market. Meeting-point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime browsing; less curated and more functional than Camden Passage.
venues.capacity_notes: Long street-market format; easy to split and regroup.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:30 or Sunday with farmers' market overlap.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is old-school everyday Islington rather than a polished destination. That gives it warmth and social texture, though the visual curation is uneven. Best as a local-life market event, not an aesthetic showcase.
venues.anecdote_story: Historic street market dating to the late nineteenth century; still managed through Islington's street-trading framework.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fruit, vegetables, fish, plants, household goods, street food and local stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Angel/Pentonville corridor; good transit access.
venues.risks_watchouts: Quality varies and recent local concern about market vitality means do not oversell it.

venues.opening_hours: London Farmers' Markets and Islington Council list every Sunday 10:00-14:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers' market; N/A for market. Meeting-point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for Sunday morning food-led browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor market; weather matters.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer focus clears the PAINTS market threshold. It is conversational because stalls can talk provenance: farms, cheese, meat, seasonal produce. The setting is less pretty than Camden Passage but operationally strong.
venues.anecdote_story: One of London Farmers' Markets' established sites, now in its third decade and located on Chapel Market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, meat, cheese, baked goods and prepared food depending on stall rotation.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime market with local family/community footfall.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not all stallholders attend weekly; weather can thin atmosphere.

venues.opening_hours: Islington Life lists Mon-Fri 09:00-18:00; food stall core is typically weekday lunch and must be host-confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street/food market; N/A for market. Meeting point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a weekday lunch walk, less ideal for slow weekend browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy lunch street; group should meet off the main pinch point.
venues.why_fits_paints: A handsome Clerkenwell street with independent food, design and cafe energy. It is a little too food-lunch-led for Tier A market days, but the street history and architecture help. Works best as a weekday cultural lunch format.
venues.anecdote_story: A nineteenth-century market street revived as a semi-pedestrian Clerkenwell food and independent-business strip.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, independent restaurants, retail and cafe frontage.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Clerkenwell/Farringdon footfall is strong; evening use should become drinks/dining, not market.
venues.backup_walk_time: 15 min by bus/tube or longer walk; within relaxed market transit backup rule
venues.risks_watchouts: Market atmosphere is weekday-lunch dependent; verify stall presence.

venues.opening_hours: Open-access canal towpath; daytime only under PAINTS v6. Exact closures/works UNVERIFIED - host must confirm on Canal & River Trust/TfL before event.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access route; N/A for walk. Meeting-point cafe near Camden/King's Cross walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight, single-file pinch points require host pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Towpath narrows; host should set regroup stops away from cyclists.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the only clearly PAINTS-permitted Islington creative walk route. It gives canal infrastructure, old industrial edges and Angel arrival as a natural story arc. Stops: Camden Lock, St Pancras/Kings Cross canal architecture, Battlebridge Basin/London Canal Museum, Islington Tunnel/Angel.
venues.anecdote_story: Regent's Canal carried industrial London goods before becoming a public urban ribbon; the Islington Tunnel explains why the route briefly disappears underground near Angel.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Canal architecture, locks, basins, industrial heritage, boat life and tunnel infrastructure.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved non-park route: Regent's Canal Camden to Angel, managed urban stretch, daytime only. Do not use after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Weather backup within about 5 min of Battlebridge Basin mid/start variant; if starting in Camden, nearest qualifying weather backup must be host-confirmed.
venues.risks_watchouts: Route is partly outside Islington and towpath cycling can make group facilitation tricky; no Islington park substitute is permitted.

venues.venue_name: Estorick Collection of Modern Italian Art
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists current exhibition Emilio Isgro: Erasing to Create, 20 May-6 Sep 2026; opening hours must be host-confirmed for event date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum/gallery admission normally supports walk-in visits, but timed/group handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong fit; compact galleries suit slow looking and discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Small museum in Georgian house; avoid talks/private views.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu/Fri late morning or Sunday afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Estorick is almost purpose-built for PAINTS: intimate, visually focused and full of art-historical hooks. The 2026 Isgro show gives a current exhibition strong enough to justify the outing. The cafe/bookshop helps the event breathe.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum opened in 1998 in a restored Grade II-listed Georgian house and is known internationally for Italian Futurist and modern Italian works.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Modern Italian art; Emilio Isgro retrospective, plus Futurist/figurative works and sculpture.
venues.safety_notes: Canonbury Square/Highbury approach is calm in daytime; use main roads after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Press within last 3 months is stronger from listings than criticism; host should confirm exhibition date and group entry.

venues.opening_hours: Official exhibitions page and New Exhibitions list Shahzia Sikander: High Seas; Closed Skies until 31 Jul 2026; hours/admission for event date UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Commercial gallery admission usually walk-in/free during open hours; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent if not during private view or install.
venues.capacity_notes: Large contemporary gallery; check if canalside garden/upper galleries are open.
venues.why_fits_paints: A major contemporary gallery hidden on a canal-side Islington street gives the exact mix of polish and discovery. The current Sikander exhibition offers paintings/works with enough visual density for group interpretation. It is famous in art circles but still feels found.
venues.anecdote_story: Victoria Miro founded the gallery in 1985 and built one of London's most influential contemporary programmes from this converted Wharf Road site.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current contemporary art exhibitions; Shahzia Sikander listed through July 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Wharf Road is quiet; use daylight or early evening and direct routes to Angel/Old Street.
venues.risks_watchouts: Between-show closure/install periods are common; host must verify before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sat 11:00-18:30 and current exhibition The Myth of Man, Daniel Hosego, 25 Jun-25 Jul 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Commercial gallery likely walk-in during open hours; group 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a short gallery anchor paired with coffee/drinks nearby.
venues.capacity_notes: Small gallery; stagger looking and avoid opening nights.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a compact Upper Street gallery with current painting/drawing-led programming. It is easy to pair with Estorick or Camden Passage. The show-specific hook is good, though press depth is lighter than the top picks.
venues.anecdote_story: Independent gallery on Upper Street showing contemporary figurative and narrative work.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Daniel Hosego, The Myth of Man, 25 Jun-25 Jul 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Upper Street daytime/evening footfall and transport are strong.
venues.backup_venue: Estorick Collection of Modern Italian Art
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-gallery install gaps; verify exhibition open to public.

venues.opening_hours: Pangolin page lists Wed-Sat 10:00-18:00 for gallery; Kings Place page lists main gallery Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00. Bank holidays/installation UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery is public during open hours; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good: sculpture supports discussion and Kings Place circulation handles groups.
venues.capacity_notes: Mezzanine/public-space exhibitions can be affected by events.
venues.why_fits_paints: One of London's few sculpture-focused galleries gives PAINTS a material, tactile angle. Kings Place's canal setting makes it easy to pair with a walk or coffee. It is cultural without feeling institutional.
venues.anecdote_story: Affiliated with Pangolin Editions foundry, the gallery specialises in modern and contemporary sculpture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Modern and contemporary sculpture, gallery and public-space displays.
venues.safety_notes: King's Cross/York Way is high-footfall by day; canal edges should be managed after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Access may be restricted during Kings Place events; verify before use.

venues.opening_hours: New Exhibitions lists Wed-Sat 12:00-18:00 and Okiki Akinfe: RM9 5AN, 23 May-8 Aug 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free contemporary gallery; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if the group is quiet and host-led; compact space.
venues.capacity_notes: Small non-profit gallery; avoid private views and install.
venues.why_fits_paints: PEER has serious non-profit credibility and a neighbourhood embeddedness that fits PAINTS. It is less obviously Islington to attendees because Hoxton reads east, but administratively and culturally it belongs in the borough edge. Exhibition specificity keeps it viable.
venues.anecdote_story: Artist-facing non-profit gallery on Hoxton Street with a public programme and neighbourhood orientation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Okiki Akinfe: RM9 5AN, 23 May-8 Aug 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Hoxton Street daytime is suitable; use main Old Street/Hoxton routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: 14 min walk; outside strict 10 min so primary is Tier B
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup exceeds 10 min walking rule; operationally weaker.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Our Space, 1 May to July 2026; museum standard hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public local museum; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for local-history conversation, less strong for paintings/craft threshold.
venues.capacity_notes: Small museum/archive environment.
venues.why_fits_paints: The current exhibition about women's experiences of Blackstock Road and Finsbury Park has strong local relevance. It is more social-history than object-beauty, which keeps it Tier B for Museum & Gallery Days. Good for a district-rooted PAINTS session.
venues.anecdote_story: A council-run local history museum preserving Islington's social, political and neighbourhood stories.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Our Space community exhibition, local history material.
venues.safety_notes: St John Street/Angel-Farringdon route is urban and lit; daytime preferred.
venues.backup_venue: Museum of the Order of St John
venues.risks_watchouts: Category fit is weaker because exhibition is not primarily paintings/sculpture/craft.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sun 10:00-16:30 and bank holiday Mondays.
venues.walk_in_policy: Admission museum; pre-booking status and 8-12 group handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted heritage day or canal-walk weather backup.
venues.capacity_notes: Small but accessible museum in former ice warehouse.
venues.why_fits_paints: The former ice warehouse and canal objects give a tactile, story-rich museum experience. It is not an art gallery, so it sits in Tier B for this category. As backup for the canal walk, it is unusually useful.
venues.anecdote_story: Housed in a Victorian ice warehouse tied to Carlo Gatti and London's ice-cream/canal trade.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Canal history, narrowboat cabin, ice trade exhibits.
venues.safety_notes: King's Cross canal basin is lively by day; avoid towpath-led night programming.
venues.risks_watchouts: Museum category fit is heritage rather than paintings/sculpture/craft.

venues.venue_name: Museum of the Order of St John
venues.opening_hours: Official planning page lists Wed-Sat 09:30-17:00 for museum galleries and shop.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official page says visits to museum galleries are free; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for history-focused group if not relying on a guided tour.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic building; some areas may be tour-only or access-limited.
venues.why_fits_paints: St John's Gate gives the group a dramatic building and centuries of object history. It is craft/material-adjacent through manuscripts, ceremonial objects and medical heritage. Less current-exhibition-led, so Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum tells the story of the Order of St John from medieval Jerusalem/Rhodes/Malta history to modern first aid and humanitarian work.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic objects, manuscripts, ceremonial material and medical/first-aid heritage.
venues.safety_notes: Clerkenwell/Farringdon area has strong daytime access; standard urban evening caution.
venues.risks_watchouts: Does not meet the current-exhibition press requirement strongly; include as heritage backup.

venues.opening_hours: Crafts Council describes the gallery as a public space for exhibitions/events; current Islington gallery hours/exhibition UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public gallery/event space; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if exhibition is open; craft objects suit close-looking discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Programme-dependent; verify not between shows.
venues.why_fits_paints: Craft is a direct PAINTS category fit and the location is very useful near Angel. The issue is live programming clarity: public pages did not surface a strong current Islington exhibition page. Keep as Tier B until the host confirms what's on.
venues.anecdote_story: The national development body for craft uses the Pentonville Road gallery to tell stories of making and material practice.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft exhibitions and events, programme-dependent.
venues.safety_notes: Pentonville Road/Angel is busy and well connected; avoid late loitering.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition not clearly verified from public page; host must confirm before use.

venues.opening_hours: Official page and Instagram list Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 12:00-17:00; bank holiday variations must be host-confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent bookshop/cafe open to walk-ins; 8-12 two-hour seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong category fit, but group seating may be limited; best as browse plus coffee in smaller clusters.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop and cafe/bar; avoid occupying all seats at peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the cleanest Islington Books & Coffee fit: actual books, actual cafe/bar, and a neighbourhood literary identity. It is specific enough to avoid generic coffee-shop syndrome. Highbury Barn also gives useful geographic counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: Ink@84 is a local independent bookshop, cafe and bar on Highbury Park, known for books plus drinks rather than books as decor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Curated books, coffee, cafe-bar drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Highbury Barn is residential and calm by day; plan main-road departures after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: 18 min walk; no qualifying same-category backup within 10 min found, so host should treat as operational caveat
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup exceeds 10-min rule; venue remains Tier A on intrinsic fit but host should pre-confirm seating.

venues.opening_hours: Official page confirms Islington bookshop/social space serving wine and coffee; exact daily hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public-facing bookshop/wine bar; group walk-in and 2-hour seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if downstairs/quiet seating is available; host must avoid event nights.
venues.capacity_notes: Two-floor space per listings, but events can take over.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday 11:00 coffee or early evening before events.
venues.why_fits_paints: BookBar makes the social premise explicit: books as a reason to gather. Coffee and wine on site satisfy the category without workaround. It is slightly buzzy, but still independent and literary.
venues.anecdote_story: BookBar was built around bringing people together through books, with readings, book clubs and a bar/cafe model.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, coffee, wine, literary events and recommendations.
venues.safety_notes: Blackstock Road has daytime footfall; Finsbury Park itself is on PAINTS removed list, so do not route meeting points through the park.
venues.backup_walk_time: 18 min walk; no qualifying same-category backup within 10 min found
venues.risks_watchouts: Finsbury Park is explicitly removed from PAINTS Creative Walk use; keep the event inside the venue/main streets.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 marks Clissold Park as PAUSED; do not source as Creative Walk or backup despite proximity.
PAINTS v6 explicitly lists Finsbury Park under Removed - do not source, use as backup, or route through.
Not on PAINTS-approved parks/routes safety list; per v6, famous/local parks not listed must be flagged for team review and not sourced.
Attractive local route but not one of the explicitly permitted v6 non-park walks; rejected for safety-list compliance.
Good independent bookshop, but public sources found did not verify on-site cafe/coffee or a literal next-door coffee partner with 2-hour seating.
Excellent literary identity but outside district and no Islington fit for this assignment.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Historically strong Islington gallery but the Wharf Road gallery closed; current foundation activity is not a reliable walk-in Islington venue.
